title: Addendum to pandemic border measures privacy impact assessment – Temporary orders (completed March 2023)  - Canada.ca
content:
## Addendum to pandemic border measures privacy impact assessment – Temporary orders (completed March 2023)
The Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) administers the Border and Travel Health Program and the Quarantine Act . Through the authority provided in that Act, the Branch works to prevent the importation of communicable diseases into Canada by screening and assessing travellers for communicable diseases at international ports of entry by implementing public health measures when communicable diseases of significance are identified, and by collaborating with partners such as the Canada Border Services Agency, to administer the Quarantine Act , 24 hours per day, 7 days per week.

## Description of (Program/Initiative)
In line that mandate PHAC implemented the temporary pre-departure covid-19 test requirements for air travellers entering Canada from the People's Republic of China, Hong Kong or Macao. Such travellers were required to provide to the aircraft operator evidence of a COVID-19 molecular test or antigen test indicating that
 they received:
- a negative result for a COVID-19 molecular test or a COVID-19 antigen test that was performed on a specimen collected no more than two days, or within another period set out under the Aeronautics Act, before the flight's initial scheduled departure time; or
- a positive result for a COVID-19 molecular test or a COVID-19 antigen test that was performed on a specimen collected at least 10 days and no more than 90 days before the flight's initial scheduled departure time.
These measures were repealed in March, 2023.

## Why was a Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) completed?
This privacy impact assessment (PIA) was an addendum to the original PIA that focused on the border measures that were in place while the pandemic emergency orders were in place (February 2020 to October 2022)as those measures constituted substantial modifications to existing programs or activities where personal information was used for an administrative purpose and because some program activities were contracted to third parties. This addendum was required because travellers' personal information was used for or was intended to be used as part of a decision-making process that directly affected the traveller.
This PIA focused on Health Canada's collection, use, disclosure, retention and disposition of personal information involved in the Quarantine Program under the Quarantine Act .
The PIA recommended mitigation actions in the following risk areas: absence of a Personal Information Banks, amending the online privacy notice statements, documenting regular disclosures in an Information Sharing Agreement and limiting the collection of personal information.
